titleStmt.title: SII 22.136: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 136. (A.R. No. 136 of 1906) SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR. GRĀMĀRDHANĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL. MĀR. ŚRĪBALADĒVA (ŚRĪVALLABHA)—5th YEAR.

summary.p: The details of date are given as Makara, ba. 9, Monday, Svāti corresponding to A.D. 1312, January 3, if the date of accession was A.D. 1307. This records an endowment of about 808 kuḻi of land after purchase by one Kāḷattinādar son of Kachchiyarāya of Paruvūr, for burning two perpetual lamps in the temple of Ūrbāgaṅkoṉḍaruḷiya-Nāyanār at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr alias Śōḻakēraḷa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . komāṟapanmar tribhuvaṉac cakravattikaḷ śrīpalatevarkku yāṇṭu 5vatu makaranāyaṟṟu Aparapakṣattu
navamiyum tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa coti nāḷ malāṭākiya ceṉaṉātavaḷa nāṭṭup pāṉūrkkūṟṟattu brahmadeyam Iṟaivāṉaraiyūrāṉa śrī
coḻakeraḷac caturvedimaṅkalattu Uṭaiyār Ūrpākaṅ koṇṭaruḷiya nāyaṉārkkut tirununtāviḷakku Erippatākap paruūr Uṭaiyār tevar kacciyarāya piḷḷai
kaḷil kāḷattinātar vaitta tiru(nu)nuntā viḷakku Iraṇṭukku Ik koyilit tāṉattār cantrātityavaraikku Erippatākak koṇṭuviṭṭa Ivvūr mel
piṭākai vittācca maṅkalattu Erikiḻ naiñcai nilattu vatikku teṟku bhārgavaṉ Āṇṭaperumāḷāṉa civapātapaṭṭar Ūrpākaṅko
ṇṭārpakkal koṇṭuṭaiyeṉāṉa peruvāykkālukkuk kiḻakku puḷiyañ ceyttaṭi- 1 Aḷaviṉpaṭi kuḻi 3 100 4 10 3va kiṇaṟu maramum
Itaṉ vaṭakku teṟkil kaṭampaṉ taṭi 4l Aḷaviṉpaṭi kuḻi 3 100 2 10 4nta- m Āka kuḻi 5 100 6 10 8- m Āritaṉ piḷḷaiyāḻviyāṉa ciṅkap pi
rāṉpaṭṭar nampiḷḷaipakkal koṇṭuṭaiyeṉāṉa Araikkāl taṭi- 1 Aḷaviṉpaṭi kuḻi 2 100 4 10- m kiṇaṟu moṭṭaiyum Āka mikutikkuṟaivu
ḷḷaṭaṅkiṉa kuḻi 8 100 8- m Iṉṉilattāl varum Erinir Urimaiyum nir koṇṭapaṭiye cātaṉaṅkaḷum koyiṟpaṇṭāratte tirunāmattukkāṇi
yākak kuṭuttu yiṟaiyiliyākki ṭum vāṅkik kuṭuttuk kalveṭṭikkuṭutteṉ kāḷattinātaṉeṉ Itu panmāheśvara rakṣai
Digital edition of SII 22.136 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
